articles.title: JetBlue to reduce Newark, LaGuardia footprint as it forges ahead in Fort Lauderdale
articles.summary: JetBlue is planning to cut some employee bases at Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey and tech bases at New York's LaGuardia Airport.

articles.title: CarMax shares fall after used car retailer reports earnings beats, CEO details turnaround plan
articles.summary: Despite the beats, questions remain about the company's ability to grow and cut costs under the plan amid tougher market conditions, including margin pressure.

articles.title: Carvana’s new vehicle strategy turns dealership into ‘playground,’ test-drive center with sales all online
articles.summary: But don't expect Carvana to sell you a vehicle at one of its seven Stellantis stores, marking a stark contrast from typical franchised dealers.

articles.title: UniQure to seek FDA approval for Huntington's disease gene therapy after previous clash with agency
articles.summary: The announcement comes months after UniQure became embroiled in a public debate with FDA leaders over the clinical trial data supporting its application.

articles.title: Target, Walmart and Amazon among brands losing LGBTQ+ consumer spending, new survey says
articles.summary: LGBTQ+ consumers are shifting spending toward companies they view as supporting DEI and away from those seen as retreating, HRC survey finds.

articles.title: CNBC to wade further into live sports with 11 simulcast WNBA games
articles.summary: The simulcasts will expose the WNBA to an affluent audience on CNBC and marks a new chapter for the business news network after it spun out from Comcast.

articles.title: How Roku fits into Fox's future — and what investors are missing about the deal
articles.summary: Fox's stock dropped following its announcement that it will acquire Roku for $22 billion. Analysts still think it's a good deal.

articles.title: Rivian laying off hundreds of workers amid R2 launch
articles.summary: Rivian said Tuesday it was laying off hundreds of workers, or less than 2% of its workforce.

articles.title: NBA to begin naming winning bids for Europe teams in the coming months, deputy commissioner says
articles.summary: The NBA plans to begin naming winning bidders for 12 permanent European teams in the next 60 to 90 days, Deputy Commissioner Mark Tatum told CNBC.

articles.title: California’s counting on an IPO tax windfall. Several factors are complicating the equation
articles.summary: The IPOs of SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic could create a tax windfall for the state of California. But experts say the revenue impact may be blunted.

articles.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
articles.summary: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

articles.title: 'Reward for failure': Investor support for Target Chair Brian Cornell falls to lowest level ever
articles.summary: Target Chair Brian Cornell built the retailer into a $100 billion-plus giant, but recent struggles and underperformance have fueled investor calls for change.

articles.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
articles.summary: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.

articles.title: UPS to invest $48 million in temperature-controlled facilities amid healthcare boom
articles.summary: UPS is announcing a new investment in healthcare logistics as demand for the temperature-controlled category booms, CNBC has learned exclusively.

articles.title: Bob Iger reflects on 10 years of Shanghai Disneyland as it defies the Chinese pullback
articles.summary: Shanghai Disneyland hit 100 million cumulative visitors in 2025, according to the company. It's a relatively new but important foothold in Disney's history.
